In Loving Memory 1983 — 2024

Deaconess · Mother · Friend

Josephine Oghenetega

28 June 1983/12 October 2024 Oberiko · née Anighoro · aged forty-one
Her story
The life of every gathering ✷ A mother to many ✷ A giver to all ✷ Forever loved ✷ The life of every gathering ✷ A mother to many ✷ A giver to all ✷ Forever loved ✷

Some people walk into a room and the whole room lifts.

Josephine was one of them. When she was there, the party held. When she laughed, everyone laughed with her. She carried other people's burdens as if they were her own, and made every person around her feel seen.

This is her story. Her children will read it one day and know exactly who their mother was. So will everyone who misses her, which is everyone who knew her.

(01)BeginningsKwale, 1983

A daughter of Kwale, the ninth of eleven.

Born on the 28th of June, 1983, to Late Pa Richard and Mrs Agnes Anighoro, Josephine was the ninth of their eleven children. From her earliest years she radiated the warmth and the love for God that would shape the whole of her life.

Her childhood in Kwale was full of family. She began school at just four years old, then moved to live with her elder sister in Agbarho, continuing at Ishere Primary School and later Unity School, where she finished her secondary education.

(02)Her own wayThe go-getter

She built her own way, one determined step at a time.

Her drive showed early. She started out running a provision store, using what she earned to put herself through higher education. In 2005 she was admitted to Delta State University to study Medical Laboratory Science, earning her Bachelor's degree in 2010.

A natural businesswoman, she thrived in venture after venture, from liquid soap production to airtime distribution, meeting every opportunity with the same determination and the same success. A hard worker, always.

She was the kind of person who would make your burden hers.

The world's best Aunty

(03)Her familyA home of her own

In 2012 she married Tega Oberiko, and built the joyful home she always carried inside her.

They married on the 27th of October, 2012, and together were blessed with three beautiful children: a daughter, Olivia, and two sons, Rema and Ezra. At home, Josephine was the heart of everything, a devoted wife, mother and sister.

No family event passed without her touch. She made sure every celebration was perfectly arranged, every person felt welcome, every good thing done for her met with genuine gratitude. She was, simply, the centre everyone gathered around.

(04)For Olivia, Rema & Ezra

To her children.

Your mother loved you more than any words on a page could hold. You were her proudest work, her greatest joy, the reason behind so much of her laughter and her hard work. She wanted the whole world for you.

Keep this close. When you want to know who she was: she was warm, she was strong, she was generous, and she adored you. That never ends. It only gets carried forward, by you.

Olivia

Rema

Ezra

(05)Her faithLiving Faith, Okoriperhe

Deaconess of Living Faith, faithful to the end.

Faith was the centre of Josephine's life. An active member of Living Faith Church, Okoriperhe, she served devotedly in the Sanctuary Unit and rose to the role of Deaconess, a testament to her dedication to God's work and to her church family.

She lived what she believed: giving, serving, loving her neighbour, and never letting anyone around her feel alone.

(06)The life of the party

And oh, how she could fill a room.

Weddings, birthdays, family gatherings, she was at the centre of all of it, laughing, dancing, pulling everyone in. These are the moments she gave us. The party is quieter now. But these remain.

My second mum. My paddy for life. Always caring, always understanding.

For Mummy, with love

(07)From her niece

She was my aunt, but that word was never big enough. She was my elder sister and my mother both. She watched me grow up. She was kind, loving, supportive, caring, the person who gave and gave and never kept count, and who treasured every small kindness shown to her in return.

I used to write to her every year on her birthday. Long before I knew I would one day be writing this, I had already told her what she was to me. I am glad I did.

"Happy birthday to my second mum, my paddy for life. Always caring and understanding. I wish you long life, prosperity and blessings from God. Have fun, Ma. I love you and always will." June 2017
"Help me celebrate one of those who will make my burden hers. She deserves some accolades for being the world's best Aunty. Happy birthday, Ma. I pray your wishes and desires come through." June 2018

Since she left, things have not been the same. The gatherings are quieter. I feel the gap every single day, and so does everyone who loved her. Death is not a journey you return from. But love does not end, and neither will the memory of her.

Rest well, my second mum. Deborah Akpokighe · her niece

(08)A life in brief

Forty-one years, fully lived.

1983Born 28 June in Kwale, the ninth of eleven children of Pa Richard and Mrs Agnes Anighoro.
1987Begins her education at just four years old, later schooling in Agbarho.
2005Admitted to Delta State University to study Medical Laboratory Science.
2010Earns her Bachelor's degree. A businesswoman and hard worker throughout.
2012Marries Tega Oberiko on 27 October. Together they raise Olivia, Rema and Ezra.
 Serves faithfully at Living Faith Church, Okoriperhe, rising to Deaconess.
2024Passes peacefully on 12 October, aged 41. Loved beyond measure. Missed every day.

Until we meet again.

She reminds us still to live with love, with joy, and with generosity, the very things she carried so naturally herself. Her legacy lives on in her children, and in the heart of everyone who knew her.

"She is clothed with strength and dignity; and she shall rejoice in time to come."

Proverbs 31:25

Rest in peace, dear Josephine.
You are loved, you are cherished, and you are deeply missed.

In loving memory · made by her niece Deborah Akpokighe
